comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "validate_to single-element-array unwrap + regression tests landed on origin/main (CI+LLM-Review green). Sole unmet criterion is the circular meta-AC 'earns a signed completion-verifier PASS' (the signature is produced BY closing). Closing uncertified by design.". Session: 31cebe57e.

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Close a work ticket with the completion-verification gate enabled (verify.require_completion_verification_for_close=true).
2. For certain inputs (observed deterministically on ticket b593, a tiny pyproject metadata diff), the verify-step model emits its structured output as a top-level JSON **array** (a bare list of findings) rather than the completion_verdict **object**.
3. src/rebar/llm/structured.py validate_to() raises StructuredOutputError('structured output must be a JSON object, got list'); the workflow step 'decide@then/verify' fails and the close is blocked fail-closed.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: verifier tolerates/normalizes a single-verdict wrapped in a list (or the verify prompt/schema forces an object), producing a PASS/FAIL verdict.
- Actual: deterministic parse failure (5/5 attempts); the ticket cannot earn a signed completion attestation and must be --force-closed (left uncertified).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tolerant_parse/validate_to in src/rebar/llm/structured.py robustly handles a top-level array carrying one verdict object (unwrap), OR the completion verify step constrains output to an object.
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the 'got list' input and asserts a verdict is produced.
- [ ] Closing a previously-affected ticket earns a signed completion-verifier PASS.
compiled_state.title: completion-verifier: top-level JSON array from verify model fails to parse (validate_to 'got list')
